Scenario.title: CostaRicto Targeting Healthcare Infrastructure
Scenario.overview: Healthcare and Public Health Simple A sophisticated spear-phishing campaign targeting healthcare organizations, leveraging exploited vulnerabilities in medical equipment to compromise systems and exfiltrate sensitive patient data.

threat_actor_profile.motivation: Profit from stolen medical research and patient data

Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, User Account, Command
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Delivery Method, User Account Authentication (Mechanism), Command Execution (Mechanism)

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attacker sends meticulously crafted phishing emails impersonating trusted medical suppliers, embedding malicious hyperlinks that redirect victims to compromised websites. This exploits human error to establish initial access to internal networks.

stage.name: Execution
stage.capec: CAPEC-226: Session Credential Falsification through Manipulation
stage.description: Leveraging known zero-day vulnerabilities in hospital IoT devices, the attacker executes arbitrary code to manipulate user session credentials, granting unauthorized access to critical systems and applications.

stage.name: Impact
stage.capec: CAPEC-24: Filter Failure through Buffer Overflow
stage.description: The attacker deploys automated scripts to exfiltrate encrypted patient records and research data via covert command channels, masking malicious traffic as legitimate network activity.

Scenario.summary: A multi-stage attack compromising healthcare systems through phishing, credential exploitation, and data exfiltration, demonstrating advanced persistence and operational security techniques.
